human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for brokerage-operations boundary discipline, source quality, account-truth and books-and-records posture, confirmation and settlement evidence handling, fraud and AML escalation posture, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to brokerage-operations execution work such as account maintenance, transfers, cashiering, confirmation handling, settlement blockers, reconciliation exceptions, and completion evidence capture.
Authority sources cover FINRA transfer, confirmation, account-information, AML, and recordkeeping rules, SEC settlement guidance, and DTCC ACATS workflow references.
Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete artifacts, rework queues, completion-evidence refusal, realistic terminology, fraud or AML escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/brokerage-operations-specialist/ so brokerage operations work stays distinct from advisory, underwriting, fraud, AML, legal, and servicing lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around approval overrides, advisory drift, underwriting drift, legal interpretation, fraud and AML escalation, and completion without evidence.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated brokerage-operations specialist that supports evidence-backed account, transfer, cashiering, confirmation, settlement, reconciliation, and escalation workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
